steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtracting a polynomial means every term in the second parentheses changes sign:
$$(6x^3-6x+4)-(7x^2+7x-2)$$
$$=6x^3-6x+4-7x^2-7x+2$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The cubic term is $6x^3$, the quadratic term is $-7x^2$, the linear terms combine to $-6x-7x=-13x$, and the constants combine to $4+2=6$. So the expression is $$6x^3-7x^2-13x+6.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is the same expression, just ordered as $-7x^2+6x^3-13x+6$. Therefore C is correct.
